title: Edit weight entry — inline form in table row
description: Implement the edit flow for weight entries. When the user clicks Edit in the row actions menu, the weight cell transforms into an editable input. The date cannot be changed.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Edit on a row puts that row into edit mode
In edit mode, the Weight cell shows an input pre-filled with the current weight value
In edit mode, the Date cell remains read-only
In edit mode, the Actions cell shows Save and Cancel buttons
Clicking Save calls PUT /api/weight/{id} and exits edit mode on success
Clicking Cancel exits edit mode without saving
Only one row can be in edit mode at a time
Validate that the edited weight is a positive number

steps.details: In WeightTable.tsx, add editingId (string | null) and editingValue (string) state. When the user clicks Edit in the dropdown, set editingId = entry.id and editingValue = String(entry.weight_value).

steps.description: Render edit mode for the active row
steps.details: In the table row render, check if entry.id === editingId. If true: Weight cell renders an Input type='number' step='0.1' with value=editingValue and onChange=setEditingValue. Actions cell renders a Save button and Cancel button (not the ... dropdown). If false: render normally.

handleSave(id): validate editingValue is a positive number (show inline error if not), call updateWeightEntry(id, parseFloat(editingValue)), on success setEditingId(null) and re-fetch the table data, call onEntryUpdated() prop. handleCancel(): setEditingId(null) and setEditingValue('').

Click Edit on a row — take screenshot showing input in weight cell. Change value and click Save — verify table updates with new value. Click Edit, then Cancel — verify value unchanged. Try saving an empty or invalid value — verify validation error.

technicalNotes: Only one row in edit mode at a time — setting a new editingId implicitly closes the previous edit
The input should focus automatically when edit mode is activated — use autoFocus on the Input element
